cards.name: Willow
cards.description: Flexibility, bending without breaking, grace under pressure.
cards.text: What would it look like to bend toward this group instead of bracing against what comes?

cards.name: Birch
cards.description: Transparency, letting light through, visible vulnerability.
cards.text: Where might letting people see what you're actually feeling make the room safer for everyone?

cards.name: Banyan
cards.description: Interconnection, support structures that become new roots, collective holding.
cards.text: What would it look like to let the group's wisdom become the structure — not just yours?

cards.name: Cedar
cards.description: Protection, shelter, creating a safe enclosure.
cards.text: What would it feel like to hold the space today without needing to control what happens inside it?

cards.name: Aspen
cards.description: Shared root systems, trembling sensitivity, what moves together beneath the surface.
cards.text: What is the group already feeling that nobody has said out loud yet?

cards.name: Baobab
cards.description: Storing what is needed for hard seasons, endurance, ancient knowing.
cards.text: What have you learned from the hard times that is finally ready to be useful here?

cards.name: Japanese Maple
cards.description: Transformation, beauty in change, letting go as a form of art.
cards.text: What are you ready to release today that might be more beautiful in the letting go?

cards.name: Olive Tree
cards.description: Peace, endurance through drought, gnarled persistence.
cards.text: What if the most powerful thing you do today is simply refuse to rush?

cards.name: Hawthorn
cards.description: Threshold guardianship, boundary between worlds, protection with thorns.
cards.text: What boundary do you need to name before you walk into the room — so you can be generous once you're inside?

cards.name: Fig Tree
cards.description: Abundance, offering shade and sustenance, the invitation to gather.
cards.text: How might you create the conditions for something to emerge — without deciding in advance what it should be?

cards.name: Redwood
cards.description: Immensity supported by shallow roots, needing neighbours to stand.
cards.text: Even the tallest things in the world cannot stand alone. Their strength is in how close they grow to each other.

cards.name: Magnolia
cards.description: Blooming before the leaves arrive, courage to show up unprotected.
cards.text: Sometimes you have to open before you feel ready. The leaves will come — but the bloom has to go first.

cards.name: Mangrove
cards.description: Thriving in between — not land, not sea. Building ground where there was none.
cards.text: The most interesting work happens in the in-between places. Not solid ground, not open water — the muddy, tangled edges where something new is taking root.

cards.name: Cherry Blossom
cards.description: Brevity, beauty that does not need to last to matter.
cards.text: Not everything valuable endures. Some of the most important moments in a room last only seconds — and that is enough.

cards.name: Cork Oak
cards.description: Regeneration, giving of yourself without being destroyed, sustainable generosity.
cards.text: There is a kind of giving that does not diminish you. The bark grows back. You are not less for having offered it.

cards.name: Oak
cards.description: Strength, deep roots, steady presence across generations.
cards.text: A society grows great when old men plant trees in whose shade they shall never sit.

cards.name: Pine
cards.description: Clarity, evergreen constancy, the scent that sharpens attention.
cards.text: In the middle of the journey of our life, I found myself in a dark wood, for the straight way was lost.

cards.name: Wisteria
cards.description: Patient beauty, the reward of years of tending, cascading abundance.
cards.text: The creation of a thousand forests is in one acorn.

cards.name: Cypress
cards.description: Solitude that is not loneliness, standing sentinel, quiet witness.
cards.text: I said to my soul, be still, and wait without hope, for hope would be hope for the wrong thing.

cards.name: Bodhi Tree
cards.description: Awakening, the place where understanding arrives, sitting with not-knowing until it transforms.
cards.text: The only way to make sense out of change is to plunge into it, move with it, and join the dance.
